## Resume Bio

ML Engineer with 6 years of experience in ML model development and deployment and scalable data pipelines and ETL systems. M.Sc. in Artificial Intelligence from Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam (Aug. 2025, GPA 8.2/10). Databricks Certified Data Engineer Professional. Eager to bring these skills to kaiko.ai.

## Core Experience Included In The Resume

### GLP Technology - ML Engineer and Team Lead

- First data hire at a consumer lending startup
- Owned end-to-end ML lifecycle for credit scoring
- Built PySpark ETL pipelines over the loan lifecycle
- Implemented data quality and schema validation controls

### Baiquan Investment - Quantitative Researcher

- Built event-driven backtesting infrastructure
- Engineered daily factor computation pipelines
- Designed cross-source data validation and alerting

### Ele.me - Data Analyst

- Built user segmentation and A/B testing analysis
- Improved reactivation performance and reporting efficiency

## Projects Included In The Resume

- 150+ training runs across 5 UQ methods on SLURM
- reproducible benchmarking pipeline

### Financial Data Lakehouse

- Databricks + Spark + AWS
- Delta Lake and schema evolution

### Expedia Hotel Recommendation System

- ranking / recommendation modeling
- large behavioral dataset

## Cover Letter Core Message

The submitted cover letter made one central argument:

`This role combines evaluation rigor with production engineering, which is exactly what my career has prepared me for.`

It specifically connected:

- thesis benchmarking and statistical testing
- GLP data quality and credit scoring reliability
- trustworthy systems in high-stakes settings

## Cover Letter Closing Positioning

The final paragraph framed your fit as:

- evaluation rigor plus production reliability
- high-stakes data mindset
- Amsterdam-based and available immediately

## What To Be Consistent About In The Interview

Stay aligned with the submitted materials:

- Do not drift into a generic "LLM builder" story.
- Stay anchored on evaluation, reliability, data quality, and reproducibility.
- Keep Databricks / Spark as supporting evidence, not the whole story.
